pi_items.metadata_value: USGS Earthquake Hazards Program FDSN Web Service
pi_items.verified_value: USGS Earthquake Hazards Program FDSN Web Service
pi_items.source: Confirmed in setup_task.sh which dynamically fetches from earthquake.usgs.gov/fdsnws/event/1/query.
Confirmed by API parameters in setup_task.sh: minlatitude=32&maxlatitude=37&minlongitude=-121&maxlongitude=-115.
Confirmed by API parameters in setup_task.sh: starttime=2023-01-01&endtime=2023-12-31.
Verified programmatically; the Aki-Utsu maximum likelihood estimate for the dataset's b-value is ~1.0. Furthermore, verifier.py accepts a b-value in the range [0.5, 1.5].

The live USGS API endpoint returns exactly 367 event data rows matching the query criteria, well over the minimum 20. Note that the script's hardcoded fallback data only provides 13 data rows, which would fall short of this minimum.

Verified against verifier.py logic, which explicitly checks for magnitude-frequency/b-value estimates (Gutenberg-Richter), depth bins/averages (Depth distribution), and temporal terms like 'jan', 'month', 'week' (Temporal clustering).

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on real earthquake data dynamically fetched from the USGS Earthquake Hazards Program FDSN Web Service. The downloaded dataset comprises 367 seismic events (M>=2.5) for Southern California (Lat 32-37, Lon -121 to -115) during the year 2023. Analysis of this exact dataset yields an empirical Gutenberg-Richter b-value of approximately 1.0, which perfectly aligns with the expected tectonic range of [0.6, 1.4]. The verifier strictly requires the spreadsheet to include a Gutenberg-Richter analysis, a Depth distribution analysis, and Temporal clustering, precisely matching the metadata claims.
